---
title: "Shell Geometry from Stationary Detector Thermality in Static KMS Spacetimes"
subtitle: "Mathematical physics / QFT in curved spacetime"
date: 2026-04-07
categories: [gen2, mathematical, submitted]
target-journal: "General Relativity and Gravitation"
status: "Submitted"
repo: "the-omega-institute/grg-shell-geometry-from-stationary-detector-thermality"
theorems: 122
featured: false
video: "https://github.com/the-omega-institute/grg-shell-geometry-from-stationary-detector-thermality/releases/download/media-v1/grg_shell_geometry_from_video.mp4"
slides: "https://github.com/the-omega-institute/grg-shell-geometry-from-stationary-detector-thermality/releases/download/media-v1/grg_shell_geometry_from_slides.pdf"
podcast: "https://github.com/the-omega-institute/grg-shell-geometry-from-stationary-detector-thermality/releases/download/media-v1/grg_shell_geometry_from_podcast.wav"
description: "Full click-record statistics of an Unruh-DeWitt detector define two codimension-one shells in static KMS spacetimes. In Schwarzschild the shell pair determines the mass parameter via a self-calibratin..."
---

::: {.paper-badges}
[General Relativity and Gravitation]{.badge-journal}
[Submitted]{.badge-status-submitted}
[122 Theorems]{.badge-theorems}
[Mathematical physics / QFT in curved spacetime]{.badge-scope}
:::

## Abstract

Full click-record statistics of an Unruh-DeWitt detector define two codimension-one shells in static KMS spacetimes. In Schwarzschild the shell pair determines the mass parameter via a self-calibrating two-mode ratio law.

**Keywords:** Unruh-DeWitt detectors, KMS states, black hole thermodynamics, symbolic dynamics, Hawking radiation

## Infographic

![grg_shell_geometry_from Infographic](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/Omega-paper-series/releases/download/papers-media-v1/grg_shell_geometry_from_infographic.png)

## Video Explainer

{{< video https://github.com/the-omega-institute/grg-shell-geometry-from-stationary-detector-thermality/releases/download/media-v1/grg_shell_geometry_from_video.mp4 >}}

::: {.media-downloads}
[📊 Slides](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/grg-shell-geometry-from-stationary-detector-thermality/releases/download/media-v1/grg_shell_geometry_from_slides.pdf)
[🎧 Podcast](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/grg-shell-geometry-from-stationary-detector-thermality/releases/download/media-v1/grg_shell_geometry_from_podcast.wav)
[🎬 Video](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/grg-shell-geometry-from-stationary-detector-thermality/releases/download/media-v1/grg_shell_geometry_from_video.mp4)
[🖼️ Infographic](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/Omega-paper-series/releases/download/papers-media-v1/grg_shell_geometry_from_infographic.png)
:::

## Paper & Source

- [Full Paper (source in this repo)](../../papers/grg-shell-geometry-from-stationary-detector-thermality/)
- [Machine-verified proofs (Lean 4)](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/automath/tree/dev-automation-integration/lean4)
- [Automath Engine](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/automath)
